html:
Exclusive to:
Only during the Event: Year of the Dragonfly
During Boat Racer, you need to touch the Start Tower and wait for the countdown. After reporting to 3, a race will begin in which players must touch each Race Checkpoint with their boats and then return back to the Start Tower. The first one to do this wins and receives several Red Pouchs. After the first player touches the Start Tower, a timer will begin, during which the other boats that touched all the Race Checkpoints must return to the zone to receive a prize. Otherwise, the player receives nothing.

For greater convenience, an arrow appears on the ship indicating the nearest Race Checkpoint which the ship did not touch. As soon as you touch the Race Checkpoint, a flyer will appear on it in the color of the arrow on your ship

Contents

  • 1 Start Tower
  • 2 Race Checkpoint
  • 3 Assets
    • 3.1 Race Checkpoint
    • 3.2 Bobber
  • 4 Gallery

Start Tower

Start TowerStart Tower Kit

When you activate the Start Tower, a countdown will begin. As soon as it ends, all ships located in the limited area will receive strikes on their ships. Each ship will have a different color arrow. These arrows will point to the nearest Race Checkpoint. You must touch each Race Checkpoint and return to the Start Tower. As soon as the first player completes it, the Start Tower will begin the countdown. The first player receives a prize in the form of a Red Pouch. The remaining players who managed to complete the task and touch the Start Tower ship also receive a small prize.

Start Tower Kit allows you to place Start Towers at a greater distance.

Race Checkpoint

Race CheckpointRace Checkpoint Kit

When the race begins, the Golden Buoy opens a small panel on top, from where fire begins to flow, thereby creating light. As soon as you touch it, a flyer will appear on it the color of the arrow on your boat. Once you have touched all the Golden Buoys you will need to return to the Start Tower

Race Checkpoint Kit allows you to place Race Checkpoints at a greater distance.

Assets

Race Checkpoint

  • Place

Bobber

  • Place
  • Idle

Gallery

  • Start Tower Map icon
  • Race Checkpoint Map icon
  • Checkpoint Ribbon Blue
  • Checkpoint Ribbon Light Blue
  • Checkpoint Ribbon Green
  • Checkpoint Ribbon Orange
  • Checkpoint Ribbon Red
  • Checkpoint Ribbon Pink
  • Checkpoint Ribbon White
  • Checkpoint Ribbon Black
  • Boat Checkpoint Indicator for Charlie's Racer
  • Boat Checkpoint Indicator Blue
  • Boat Checkpoint Indicator Light Blue
  • Boat Checkpoint Indicator Green
  • Boat Checkpoint Indicator Orange
  • Boat Checkpoint Indicator Red
  • Boat Checkpoint Indicator Pink
  • Boat Checkpoint Indicator White
  • Boat Checkpoint Indicator Black

Exclusive to:
Only during the Event: Year of the Dragonfly
During Boat Racer, you need to touch the Start Tower and wait for the countdown. After reporting to 3, a race will begin in which players must touch each Race Checkpoint with their boats and then return back to the Start Tower. The first one to do this wins and receives several Red Pouchs. After the first player touches the Start Tower, a timer will begin, during which the other boats that touched all the Race Checkpoints must return to the zone to receive a prize. Otherwise, the player receives nothing.
For greater convenience, an arrow appears on the ship indicating the nearest Race Checkpoint which the ship did not touch. As soon as you touch the Race Checkpoint, a flyer will appear on it in the color of the arrow on your ship

“You have to start somewhere.”
–Wilson, when examining a Start Tower
“Let's just get this over with.”
–Willow, when examining a Start Tower
“Is starting place for boat race.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Start Tower
“Is this the start or the end? Or the start of the end?”
–Wendy, when examining a Start Tower
“WHERE THE MISERY BEGINS”
–WX-78, when examining a Start Tower
“I do love a good old fashioned boat race.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Start Tower
“A boat race? If you want a challenge, try log rolling.”
–Woodie, when examining a Start Tower
“Can I just wait here for everyone to be done?”
–Maxwell, when examining a Start Tower
“I shall triumph, in the name of the Valyrie!”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Start Tower
“Last one back here is a rotten egg!”
–Webber, when examining a Start Tower
“A good place to start.”
–Warly, when examining a Start Tower
“Start Place”
–Wormwood, when examining a Start Tower
“No sense stallin', let's go!”
–Winona, when examining a Start Tower
“The fun has just begun!”
–Wortox, when examining a Start Tower
“This the start place.”
–Wurt, when examining a Start Tower
“So this is where we start the race.”
–Walter, when examining a Start Tower
“We should start this race on time.”
–Wanda, when examining a Start Tower
“Where to start?”
–Wilson, when examining a Start Tower Kit
“Does it HAVE to go in the water?”
–Willow, when examining a Start Tower Kit
“Where is best place for race to start?”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Start Tower Kit
“Does it matter where? It's all a game isn't it?”
–Wendy, when examining a Start Tower Kit
“I WOULD RATHER DO ANYTHING ELSE”
–WX-78, when examining a Start Tower Kit
“We must determine the best spot to start and finish the race.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Start Tower Kit
“Let's get it out on the water, eh?”
–Woodie, when examining a Start Tower Kit
“Wherever.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Start Tower Kit
“The location is unimportant. Only know that I will arrive first.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Start Tower Kit
“Should we start here? How about there? Or there?”
–Webber, when examining a Start Tower Kit
“What a big decision.”
–Warly, when examining a Start Tower Kit
“Need to plant it on the water”
–Wormwood, when examining a Start Tower Kit
“Better get started!”
–Winona, when examining a Start Tower Kit
“Shall I get started?”
–Wortox, when examining a Start Tower Kit
“Hurry up, wanna start race!!”
–Wurt, when examining a Start Tower Kit
“Hmm, I wonder where we should start the race.”
–Walter, when examining a Start Tower Kit
“Where to begin?”
–Wanda, when examining a Start Tower Kit
When you activate the Start Tower, a countdown will begin. As soon as it ends, all ships located in the limited area will receive strikes on their ships. Each ship will have a different color arrow. These arrows will point to the nearest Race Checkpoint. You must touch each Race Checkpoint and return to the Start Tower. As soon as the first player completes it, the Start Tower will begin the countdown. The first player receives a prize in the form of a Red Pouch. The remaining players who managed to complete the task and touch the Start Tower ship also receive a small prize.
Start Tower Kit allows you to place Start Towers at a greater distance.

“There's the checkpoint!”
–Wilson, when examining a Race Checkpoint
“Yup. That's a checkpoint.”
–Willow, when examining a Race Checkpoint
“Is point for checking”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Race Checkpoint
“Where do we go from here?”
–Wendy, when examining a Race Checkpoint
“A WRETCHED CHECKPOINT FOR THE STUPID RACE”
–WX-78, when examining a Race Checkpoint
“This should help us stay on course.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Race Checkpoint
“Keep your eye on the next checkpoint, Luce!”
–Woodie, when examining a Race Checkpoint
“Are we there yet?”
–Maxwell, when examining a Race Checkpoint
“Onward!”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Race Checkpoint
“Check! It's a checkpoint!”
–Webber, when examining a Race Checkpoint
“Almost there!”
–Warly, when examining a Race Checkpoint
“That way!”
–Wormwood, when examining a Race Checkpoint
“Checkpoint ahead!”
–Winona, when examining a Race Checkpoint
“There's a point in need of checking!”
–Wortox, when examining a Race Checkpoint
“It show the way!”
–Wurt, when examining a Race Checkpoint
“Hope it doesn't float away.”
–Walter, when examining a Race Checkpoint
“A progress marker.”
–Wanda, when examining a Race Checkpoint
“One more thing to check off my list.”
–Wilson, when examining a Race Checkpoint Kit
“Does this thing even float?”
–Willow, when examining a Race Checkpoint Kit
“Will find good spot, leave it to Wolfgang!”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Race Checkpoint Kit
“I should throw this in the water.”
–Wendy, when examining a Race Checkpoint Kit
“DO I HAVE TO”
–WX-78, when examining a Race Checkpoint Kit
“It's placement must be strategic.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Race Checkpoint Kit
“Let's get it out on the water, eh?”
–Woodie, when examining a Race Checkpoint Kit
“Whatever.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Race Checkpoint Kit
“Chart a course for victory!”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Race Checkpoint Kit
“We get to decide where it goes?”
–Webber, when examining a Race Checkpoint Kit
“Let us throw it in the soup.”
–Warly, when examining a Race Checkpoint Kit
“Need to plant it on the water”
–Wormwood, when examining a Race Checkpoint Kit
“Better roll up my sleeves and get to buildin'.”
–Winona, when examining a Race Checkpoint Kit
“Where should I place the next stop for the race?”
–Wortox, when examining a Race Checkpoint Kit
“Gonna put it in best spot. Not telling where.”
–Wurt, when examining a Race Checkpoint Kit
“Where should we toss this, Woby?”
–Walter, when examining a Race Checkpoint Kit
“Must find the most logical spot for this.”
–Wanda, when examining a Race Checkpoint Kit

title: Boat Racer
wikitext:
{{Exclusivity|Don't Starve Together}}
{{Event|Year of the Dragonfly}}
During Boat Racer, you need to touch the Start Tower and wait for the countdown. After reporting to 3, a race will begin in which players must touch each [[#Race Checkpoint|Race Checkpoint]] with their boats and then return back to the [[#Start Tower|Start Tower]]. The first one to do this wins and receives several [[Lucky_Gold_Nugget#Dragonfly's_Red_Pouch|Red Pouch]]s. After the first player touches the Start Tower, a timer will begin, during which the other boats that touched all the Race Checkpoints must return to the zone to receive a prize. Otherwise, the player receives nothing.

For greater convenience, an arrow appears on the ship indicating the nearest Race Checkpoint which the ship did not touch. As soon as you touch the Race Checkpoint, a flyer will appear on it in the color of the arrow on your ship
